title: Q2 renewal forecast lands within 2% of committed ARR
anchor: Actual renewal ARR at $1.77M versus forecast of $1.8M, a 1.7% variance on $1.8M renewing book.
body: Actual renewal ARR at $1.77M versus forecast of $1.8M, a 1.7% variance on $1.8M renewing book. Trailing four-quarter forecast variance averaged 8.2%.
trace: Q2 2026 forecast accuracy represents significant improvement over the 8.2% trailing average, indicating better renewal predictability.

title: Mid-market NRR climbs to 112% in Q2 from 105% trailing average
anchor: Mid-market segment NRR at 112% across $1.116M renewing book, up from trailing three-quarter average of 104.7%.
connect: Multi-product customers within mid-market drove expansion at 124% NRR versus 102% for single-product.
body: Mid-market segment NRR at 112% across $1.116M renewing book, up from trailing three-quarter average of 104.7%. Multi-product customers within mid-market drove expansion at 124% NRR versus 102% for single-product.
trace: Mid-market NRR acceleration corresponds with multi-product adoption driving the expansion engine within this segment.

title: Multi-product customers expand at 2.4x rate of single-product base
anchor: Multi-product accounts delivered 124% NRR in Q2 across 46 accounts versus 102% across 74 single-product customers.
connect: Multi-product share of customer base sits at 38.3%.
body: Multi-product accounts delivered 124% NRR in Q2 across 46 accounts versus 102% across 74 single-product customers. Multi-product share of customer base sits at 38.3%.
trace: The NRR gap between multi-product and single-product customers shows the compounding effect of product breadth on expansion revenue.

title: Atlas Insights adoption correlates with mid-market expansion activity
anchor: 18 of 24 mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded ARR during Q2.
connect: Mid-market segment overall delivered 112% NRR in Q2.
body: 18 of 24 mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded ARR during Q2. Mid-market segment overall delivered 112% NRR in Q2.
trace: Atlas Insights usage within the mid-market tier shows strong correlation with expansion behavior, representing 75% expansion rate among adopters.

title: At-risk ARR concentration drops 29% month over month
anchor: April at-risk pool at $220K ARR across 3 accounts versus March at $310K across 7 accounts.
connect: Top-20-ARR accounts in at-risk status decreased from 5 to 2.
body: April at-risk pool at $220K ARR across 3 accounts versus March at $310K across 7 accounts. Top-20-ARR accounts in at-risk status decreased from 5 to 2.
trace: At-risk pool reduction driven by 4 accounts resolving without new additions, with concentration shifting away from highest-value accounts.

title: Time to first value accelerates 39% in mid-market new cohort
anchor: Q2 mid-market new customers reached first value at 23 days across 24 accounts versus 38 days in Q1 across 22 accounts.
connect: Q1 cohort under-30-day TTFV achieved 91% renewal versus 74% for over-60-day TTFV.
body: Q2 mid-market new customers reached first value at 23 days across 24 accounts versus 38 days in Q1 across 22 accounts. Q1 cohort under-30-day TTFV achieved 91% renewal versus 74% for over-60-day TTFV.
trace: TTFV improvement from Q1 to Q2 positions the newer cohort for stronger retention based on historical TTFV-to-renewal patterns.

title: Coverage tier retention divergence widens to 14 percentage points
anchor: High-touch coverage delivered 96% GRR in Q2 across 22 accounts versus tech-touch at 82% across 98 accounts.
connect: High-touch accounts represent 18.3% of customer base but carry top ARR concentration.
body: High-touch coverage delivered 96% GRR in Q2 across 22 accounts versus tech-touch at 82% across 98 accounts. High-touch accounts represent 18.3% of customer base but carry top ARR concentration.
trace: Retention divergence between coverage tiers shows the ARR protection value of high-touch coverage for top accounts.

title: Health score distribution shifts toward green across mid-market tier
anchor: Mid-market green health accounts at 78% in Q2 versus 72% in Q1, representing 39 of 50 accounts.
connect: Q1 green-health customers renewed at 97% versus yellow-health at 70%.
body: Mid-market green health accounts at 78% in Q2 versus 72% in Q1, representing 39 of 50 accounts. Q1 green-health customers renewed at 97% versus yellow-health at 70%.
trace: Health distribution improvement in mid-market aligns with segment-level NRR acceleration, with green-health showing strong renewal predictive value.

title: License utilization above 80% climbs 57% in mid-market segment
anchor: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reached 22 in Q2 versus 14 in Q1.
connect: Mid-market segment delivered 112% NRR in Q2.
body: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reached 22 in Q2 versus 14 in Q1. Mid-market segment delivered 112% NRR in Q2.
trace: License utilization increase within mid-market corresponds with the segment's NRR acceleration, suggesting usage-driven expansion readiness.

title: Customer health reviews convert expansion opportunities at 83% acceptance
anchor: March health-review-sourced expansion opportunities generated 5 accepted out of 6 submitted to the revenue team.
connect: Outbound expansion opportunities converted at 41% acceptance over the same period.
body: March health-review-sourced expansion opportunities generated 5 accepted out of 6 submitted to the revenue team. Outbound expansion opportunities converted at 41% acceptance over the same period.
trace: Health review sourcing shows 2x acceptance rate versus outbound expansion, indicating relationship-based opportunities have higher conversion quality.

title: Executive sponsor depth correlates with enterprise renewal signing
anchor: 4 of 7 enterprise renewals signed in April involved executive sponsors whose engagement deepened during Q1.
connect: Enterprise segment delivered 94% GRR and 106% NRR in Q2.
body: 4 of 7 enterprise renewals signed in April involved executive sponsors whose engagement deepened during Q1. Enterprise segment delivered 94% GRR and 106% NRR in Q2.
trace: Executive sponsor relationship development in Q1 corresponds with successful enterprise renewal execution in Q2.

title: 90-day retention improves 5 points with TTFV acceleration
anchor: Q1 new-customer cohort achieved 88% 90-day retention across 60 accounts at 26-day TTFV.
connect: Q4 cohort delivered 83% 90-day retention at 38-day TTFV across 55 accounts.
body: Q1 new-customer cohort achieved 88% 90-day retention across 60 accounts at 26-day TTFV. Q4 cohort delivered 83% 90-day retention at 38-day TTFV across 55 accounts.
trace: TTFV improvement from 38 to 26 days between Q4 and Q1 cohorts correlates with 5-point retention improvement at the 90-day mark.

title: Beacon Logistics renews three months early on $280K contract
anchor: Beacon signed $280K renewal on April 21 with original contract end date of July 15.
connect: Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15.
body: Beacon signed $280K renewal on April 21 with original contract end date of July 15. Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15.
trace: Early renewal timing aligns with product launch schedule, suggesting feature roadmap influenced renewal acceleration.

title: April expansion pipeline reaches $340K from health review sourcing
anchor: 8 expansion opportunities sourced from customer health reviews in April totaling $340K.
connect: March health reviews generated 6 opportunities with 83% acceptance rate.
body: 8 expansion opportunities sourced from customer health reviews in April totaling $340K. March health reviews generated 6 opportunities with 83% acceptance rate.
trace: Health review expansion sourcing increased from 6 to 8 opportunities month over month, maintaining the high-conversion channel.

title: Small business segment GRR trails other tiers at 86%
anchor: Small-business renewing book delivered 86% GRR on $252K ARR in Q2.
connect: Mid-market achieved 91% GRR and enterprise 94% GRR over the same period.
body: Small-business renewing book delivered 86% GRR on $252K ARR in Q2. Mid-market achieved 91% GRR and enterprise 94% GRR over the same period.
trace: Small business segment shows retention challenges relative to mid-market and enterprise, representing the lowest GRR across all tiers.

title: Top-ARR at-risk accounts schedule executive sponsor reviews
anchor: 2 of 2 top-20-ARR accounts currently at-risk have executive sponsor reviews calendared for this week.
connect: Total at-risk pool sits at $220K ARR across 3 accounts in April.
body: 2 of 2 top-20-ARR accounts currently at-risk have executive sponsor reviews calendared for this week. Total at-risk pool sits at $220K ARR across 3 accounts in April.
trace: Executive engagement coverage reaches 100% of high-value at-risk accounts, indicating escalation protocol activation.

title: Multi-product expansion advantage widens from Q1 to Q2
anchor: Multi-product NRR reached 124% in Q2 versus 116% in Q1, while single-product held at 102% both quarters.
connect: Multi-product customer base represents 38.3% of total accounts.
body: Multi-product NRR reached 124% in Q2 versus 116% in Q1, while single-product held at 102% both quarters. Multi-product customer base represents 38.3% of total accounts.
trace: Multi-product NRR acceleration from 116% to 124% while single-product remains flat indicates expanding value realization among breadth customers.

title: Coverage model retention spread reaches 14-point differential
anchor: High-touch and tech-touch GRR spread at 14 percentage points in Q2 versus 8 points in Q1.
connect: High-touch coverage serves 18.3% of customer base concentrated in top ARR accounts.
body: High-touch and tech-touch GRR spread at 14 percentage points in Q2 versus 8 points in Q1. High-touch coverage serves 18.3% of customer base concentrated in top ARR accounts.
trace: Coverage tier performance divergence widened from Q1 to Q2, with tech-touch retention declining while high-touch maintained 96% GRR.
